# TASKS: #333 — [FEAT] No home for reference algorithm spec — new optional play needed between prepare and implement
<!-- Generated by start-feature-planning | Plan sub-agent output -->

**Intent:** Implement the spec through granular, dependency-ordered tasks — from authoring intent.yaml and the skill, through play compilation, review, and scenario validation, to final registration in the playbook catalog.

> **Note:** T1, T2, T3… are informal planning labels for this task breakdown. They are NOT plan.yaml milestone IDs (which use the T-001, T-002 format).

---

### Dependency Graph

```
T1 (author intent.yaml) ─────┐
                              ├─► T3 (compile play) ─► T4 (review SKILL.md) ─► T5 (scenario validation) ─► T6 (evidence schema check) ─► T7 (register in playbook-catalog)
T2 (author skill) ───────────┘
```

T1 and T2 are independent of each other and can be authored in parallel. T3 depends on both.

---

### Task Breakdown

#### T1: Author `core/components/plays/algorithm/reference/intent.yaml`
- **Files:**
  - `core/components/plays/algorithm/reference/intent.yaml` — create
- **Details:**
  - Create the directory `core/components/plays/algorithm/reference/`
  - Author intent.yaml following the schema from `prepare/reference/intent.yaml` and `implement/reference/intent.yaml`
  - Set `workflow: direct-generation` (Structure B)
  - Encode constraints C1-C17, failure conditions F1-F7, and four scenarios (graceful skip, full execution, explicit flag override, implement-after-play)
  - C14 must encode the `evidence.record` gate: read from `.garura/core/config.yaml` (default `true`); when false, skip evidence file, status file, and self-commit — user summary always shown
  - Set `agents: [tech-architect]`, `skills: [draft-reference-algorithms]`, `optional: true`
  - Version: 1.0.0
- **Depends on:** none
- **Expected Outcome:** `reference/intent.yaml` exists with all constraints, failure conditions, and scenarios encoded
- **Verification:** `cat core/components/plays/algorithm/reference/intent.yaml` — presence of C1-C17, F1-F7 keys

---

#### T2: Author `core/components/skills/draft-reference-algorithms/SKILL.md`
- **Files:**
  - `core/components/skills/draft-reference-algorithms/SKILL.md` — create
- **Details:**
  - Create the directory `core/components/skills/draft-reference-algorithms/`
  - Model the skill on `core/components/skills/draft-lld/SKILL.md` as the canonical pattern
  - Skill receives: `tech_yaml_path`, `interface_ids[]`, `stm_base`, `issue`
  - Pre-check: if output path `{stm_base}/{issue}/context/design/reference-algorithms.md` exists and is LOCKED, return early
  - For each interface_id: read the `internal_interfaces` entry from tech.yaml, analyze `behavior_contracts`, produce a `## {interface_id}` section with:
    - Complexity class (state-machine | scheduler | retry-backoff | reconciliation | other)
    - `**Invariants:**` list
    - `**Edge cases:**` list
    - `**Reference pseudocode:**` fenced block (language-agnostic)
  - Write DRAFT output to `{stm_base}/{issue}/context/design/reference-algorithms.md`
  - Return output contract: `{ reference_algorithms_path: "{path}", status: "DRAFT" }`
- **Depends on:** none
- **Expected Outcome:** Skill SKILL.md exists with correct input contract, LOCKED check, DRAFT output, and return contract
- **Verification:** Read SKILL.md — confirm input fields, LOCKED check present, output path matches C6

---

#### T3: Compile play via `/create-play --build algorithm`
- **Files:**
  - `core/components/plays/algorithm/SKILL.md` — create (generated by create-play)
- **Details:**
  - Run `/create-play --build algorithm` (the create-play skill)
  - The create-play workflow executes G1-G11 gate checks including P1-P11 agent audit for tech-architect
  - Workflow selection: Structure B (direct-generation) confirmed from intent.yaml
  - Compiled SKILL.md must include: Frontmatter, Header, Compiled From, Role + Agent Boundaries table, Pre-flight, Task DAG, Workflow, Scenario Validation, Evidence & Close
  - Compiled SKILL.md pre-flight must include: all 11 complexity indicators (C3), `algorithm_spec_required: true` check (C4), graceful skip path (F4)
  - Compiled SKILL.md workflow must include: tech-architect JSON dispatch contract with `tech_yaml_path` and `interface_ids` in stm.input
- **Depends on:** T1, T2
- **Expected Outcome:** `core/components/plays/algorithm/SKILL.md` exists; create-play reports no gate failures
- **Verification:** `ls core/components/plays/algorithm/SKILL.md` — exists; grep for `## Task DAG` — present

---

#### T4: Review compiled SKILL.md
- **Files:** read-only
- **Details:**
  - Read the compiled `core/components/plays/algorithm/SKILL.md`
  - Verify checklist (AC3-AC10 from verify.md):
    - `## Compiled From` present referencing intent.yaml
    - `## Task DAG` present
    - Pre-flight has all 11 indicator terms
    - Pre-flight has graceful skip (exit 0) when qualifying_interfaces is empty
    - tech-architect dispatch block has `tech_yaml_path` + `interface_ids` in stm.input
    - Output path is `context/design/reference-algorithms.md`
    - No Write operations targeting prepare-owned artifacts (tech.yaml, plan.yaml, epic-spec.yaml, scenarios.yaml)
    - Evidence path `evidence/algorithm/reference-algorithms-summary.yaml` defined
    - Evidence & Close section reads `evidence.record` from `.garura/core/config.yaml` and gates evidence/status/self-commit on that flag; user summary unconditional
- **Depends on:** T3
- **Expected Outcome:** All AC3-AC16 checklist items confirmed; any gaps sent back to T3 for re-compile
- **Verification:** Human reviewer sign-off; verification notes recorded

---

#### T5: Scenario validation
- **Files:** mock test artifacts (temporary, not committed)
- **Details:**
  - Execute all 4 scenarios from verify.md (CRUD skip, state machine detection, explicit flag, implement-after)
  - For Scenarios 1-3: create mock tech.yaml files with appropriate `internal_interfaces` content
  - For Scenario 4: confirm implement SKILL.md pre-flight does not assert closed list for context/design/
  - Document results per scenario: pass/fail, actual vs expected behavior
- **Depends on:** T4
- **Expected Outcome:** All 4 scenarios pass; results documented
- **Verification:** Scenario results file written to `{stm_base}/333/context/` or noted inline

---

#### T6: Evidence schema check
- **Files:** read-only
- **Details:**
  - Confirm that the evidence artifact `algorithms-summary.yaml` defined in SKILL.md uses fields consistent with ADR 016 evidence convention
  - Required fields per ADR 016: `play`, `issue`, `status`, plus play-specific fields
  - Reference-algorithm adds: `qualifying_interfaces[]`, `artifact` (path), `locked` (bool)
  - Cross-check against evidence artifacts from other plays (e.g., prepare evidence) to confirm schema consistency
- **Depends on:** T5
- **Expected Outcome:** Evidence schema confirmed consistent with ADR 016; no schema violations
- **Verification:** Compare field names against one existing evidence artifact from `{stm_base}/*/evidence/*/`

---

#### T7: Register in `docs/design/playbook-catalog.md`
- **Files:**
  - `docs/design/playbook-catalog.md` — modify (or create if it doesn't exist yet)
- **Details:**
  - Add `algorithm` as an optional play between `prepare` and `implement` in the pipeline sequence
  - Include: play name, description, optional flag, inputs (tech.yaml), outputs (algorithms.md), consumption pathway note (human-normative guidance in context package; not programmatically consumed by implement in v1)
  - Add future work item: "When implement's intent.yaml adds nullable `reference_algorithms_path` to tech-designer stm.input, code-builder will receive pseudocode alongside contracts"
  - If playbook-catalog.md does not exist, check `docs/` for a similar index file before creating
- **Depends on:** T6
- **Expected Outcome:** Pipeline sequence in docs reflects the new optional play
- **Verification:** `grep "algorithm" docs/design/playbook-catalog.md` — entry present
